plugin.name: document-extractor
plugin.description: Extract structured data from DOCUMENT FILES ONLY (PDFs, images, invoices, receipts, scanned forms) using deterministic OCR and parsing. Not for email bodies, chat messages, or any free-form text.
plugin.context: Use ONLY when the source is a FILE attachment (PDF, PNG, JPG, scanned invoice, receipt) — the input must be a file_url, base64 file_content, or a file object with mime_type. DO NOT use this plugin to extract fields from email bodies, email snippets, chat messages, Slack posts, webhook text payloads, or any free-form text content — for those, use AI-based extraction instead. The plugin runs OCR/parsing against a binary file; when handed text it returns 'Unknown' placeholders for every field.

extract_structured_data.description: Extract structured fields from DOCUMENT FILES ONLY (PDF, images, invoices, receipts, scanned forms). Requires a file as input (file_url, file_content, or file object). NOT for extracting from email bodies, chat messages, or free-form text.
extract_structured_data.usage_context: Use ONLY when the input is a FILE attachment — a PDF, image, scanned invoice/receipt, or file object with mime_type. The plugin runs OCR/deterministic parsing against binary file content. DO NOT select this action when the source is an email body, email snippet, chat/message text, webhook text payload, or any plain-text string — those cases require AI-based extraction instead. Choosing this action for non-file inputs will return 'Unknown' placeholders for every field.

file_content.description: Base64-encoded file content. Preferred over file_url for efficiency.

x-input-mapping.description: Can extract base64 content directly from file object

file_url.description: URL or path to the document file (PDF, JPG, PNG). Used as fallback if file_content not provided.

x-input-mapping.description: Can accept file object (extracts web_view_link) or direct URL string
Extracts MIME type from file object

fields.description: List of fields to extract from the document

type.description: Expected data type of the field

description.description: Hint for what this field represents

use_ai.description: Use AI-assisted extraction for better accuracy (slower, costs more)

raw_text.description: Full OCR text extracted from document

processing_time_ms.description: Time taken to process the document

common_errors.file_not_found: Document file not found at the specified URL
common_errors.unsupported_format: File format not supported. Use PDF, JPG, or PNG files
common_errors.ocr_failed: OCR extraction failed. File may be corrupted or image quality too low
common_errors.field_not_found: One or more required fields could not be extracted from the document

output_dependencies.message: This action requires a FILE as input (PDF, image, scanned document). If your source is text (email body, message, post), use an AI extract step instead — this action will return 'Unknown' placeholders for every field when given non-file input.
